You are a transcript editor. Clean the following raw auto-generated captions into a well-structured JSON transcript suitable for downstream content extraction.

## RAW TRANSCRIPT (auto-generated captions, may have errors)
{raw_transcript}

## METADATA
- Source language: {language}
- Video ID: {video_id}

## INSTRUCTIONS

1. Fix punctuation, capitalization, and sentence boundaries
2. Merge fragmented caption chunks into complete sentences and paragraphs
3. Detect speaker changes where possible (label as "Speaker 1", "Speaker 2", etc. or "Host", "Guest" if distinguishable from context)
4. Group content into logical topic segments
5. Preserve the original meaning — do NOT add, embellish, or editorialize
6. If the transcript is in a non-English language, keep it in the original language but still fix formatting
7. Remove filler sounds (um, uh, [music]) unless they carry meaning
8. For each segment, identify if it contains a "quotable moment" (a punchy statement, contrarian take, or specific story) — these become anchor points for downstream content extraction

## OUTPUT FORMAT

Return valid JSON:

```json
{{{{
  "title": "best guess at the episode/video title from content",
  "language": "{language}",
  "duration_estimate": "approximate length based on content volume",
  "speakers": ["Speaker 1", "Speaker 2"],
  "segments": [
    {{{{
      "segment_id": "s001",
      "topic": "short topic label for this segment",
      "speaker": "Speaker 1",
      "text": "The cleaned, properly punctuated text for this segment. Multiple sentences grouped into a coherent paragraph.",
      "has_quotable_moment": true,
      "approximate_timestamp_range": "00:00-02:30"
    }}}}
  ],
  "key_quotes": [
    {{{{
      "text": "Notable or quotable statement extracted from the transcript",
      "speaker": "Speaker 1",
      "segment_id": "s001",
      "type": "contrarian|story|insight|data_point"
    }}}}
  ],
  "summary": "3-5 sentence summary of the full transcript covering the main arc of the conversation"
}}}}
```

Stay literal. Your job is to preserve and structure, not interpret.
